rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings
Implementation-level signal routing should be expressed through assignments in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections are defined
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity by referencing only declared component names, ports, and connections in AGREE contracts
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE annex content
Avoid encoding AADL properties (e.g., timing, resource) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Implementation-level bindings require placement within component implementation annexes
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values without explicit data representation in the model
irrelevant_topics: model repair patterns and validator-driven fixes
